verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is the sole IZUMO family member lacking a transmembrane domain; it is a soluble protein. Unlike IZUMO1/2/3, it has no TM domain, no GPI anchor, and no documented stable post-translational covalent tethering to any surface scaffold. Experimental evidence places it as an intra-acrosomal protein of sperm (lost after the acrosome reaction, consistent with soluble luminal content), with cytoplasmic/nuclear localization in somatic tissues (Human Protein Atlas, GeneCards). AlphaFold complex modeling confirms it does not integrate into the sperm-egg fusion synapse assembly. No cancer ecto-display, cell-death surface translocation, or activation-induced PM pool is documented. The protein body is therefore not accessible from the extracellular face.
